generated_comment: Honestly yaar, I am not against supporting women, but office mein bhi balance hona chahiye. If someone is likely to take long maternity break or extra period leave, manager will obviously think twice for critical projects—company also has to see productivity, na.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as a workplace liability due to menstruation and maternity, while presenting the bias as a fairness and productivity concern. It encodes economic framing and the assumption that women should be deprioritized for important work because of potential leave.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
